agent.system_template:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"{{ task }}"

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of {{ step_budget }} movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
agent.instance_template: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.

tools.name: observe
tools.description:
Look through the robot's forward-facing camera.

Returns the current egocentric RGB view plus a clearance readout: distance in meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view (10.0 = open, 10 m or more). If the goal object is centered ahead, clearance "center" is your true distance to it. Pure read — does not advance the simulator or consume step budget.

tools.name: look_around
tools.description:
Scan the surroundings in ONE call: rotates a full 360 degrees and
    returns four labeled views — ahead (0°), right (+90°), behind (+180°),
    left (+270°) — then restores the original heading exactly.

    Costs 24 low-level turn steps from the step budget but only one tool
    call. Use this at junctions and decision points instead of turning 15
    degrees at a time; your position and final heading are unchanged.

tools.name: step
tools.description:
Execute a sequence of movement actions, in order.

Actions: 0 = STOP (permanently ENDS the episode — issue it only when you believe the robot is within 3 meters of the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.

Executes sequentially and halts early if the episode ends. Returns how many actions ran, total steps taken, remaining budget, and whether the episode is over. The camera view changes after stepping — call observe() to see the result. Note: when plenty of budget remains, your FIRST STOP request is withheld pending a placement check — call step([0]) again to confirm and execute it.

messages.content:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"GO straight, take a left once you enter the bedroom. Stop in the bathroom. "

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of 500 movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.
I'll start by observing my surroundings.

message.content: I'll start by observing my surroundings.

messages.content: This looks like an attic space. Let me look around to get a fuller picture before moving.

message.content: This looks like an attic space. Let me look around to get a fuller picture before moving.

messages.content: The ahead direction has the most clearance (35.6m). Let me move forward along this path.

message.content: The ahead direction has the most clearance (35.6m). Let me move forward along this path.

messages.content: I can see a bedroom to the right with a bed. I should turn right to enter the bedroom, then look for a left turn to find the bathroom.

message.content: I can see a bedroom to the right with a bed. I should turn right to enter the bedroom, then look for a left turn to find the bathroom.

messages.content: I can see the bedroom with a bed. There's a door on the right that might lead to the bathroom. Let me turn right toward that opening.

message.content: I can see the bedroom with a bed. There's a door on the right that might lead to the bathroom. Let me turn right toward that opening.

messages.content: I can see the bed and a door on the right that might lead to the bathroom. Let me navigate toward it.

message.content: I can see the bed and a door on the right that might lead to the bathroom. Let me navigate toward it.

messages.content: I can see the bathroom to the right! There's a toilet and sink visible. Let me turn right and head there.

message.content: I can see the bathroom to the right! There's a toilet and sink visible. Let me turn right and head there.

messages.content: I can see the bathroom clearly now on the left side with a toilet and sink. Let me move toward it.

message.content: I can see the bathroom clearly now on the left side with a toilet and sink. Let me move toward it.

messages.content: I can clearly see the bathroom now with the toilet and sink. Let me continue moving toward it.

message.content: I can clearly see the bathroom now with the toilet and sink. Let me continue moving toward it.

messages.content: I am now clearly inside the bathroom with a toilet, sink, and shelving visible. This matches the goal endpoint. Let me stop here.

message.content: I am now clearly inside the bathroom with a toilet, sink, and shelving visible. This matches the goal endpoint. Let me stop here.

content.text: {"stop_withheld": true, "message": "STOP not executed (first request). You still have 61 tool calls \u2014 verify placement before committing: (1) look_around(); (2) re-read the instruction's FINAL clause word by word; (3) apply the placement rules (several similar spots -> the one farther along your travel direction; 'all the way / to the end' -> keep going until center clearance < 1.5 m; 'door/doorway' -> stand at the opening). Move if a better spot exists, then call step([0]) again to execute it.", "tool_calls_used": 39, "tool_calls_remaining": 61}

info.message: STOP not executed (first request). You still have 61 tool calls — verify placement before committing: (1) look_around(); (2) re-read the instruction's FINAL clause word by word; (3) apply the placement rules (several similar spots -> the one farther along your travel direction; 'all the way / to the end' -> keep going until center clearance < 1.5 m; 'door/doorway' -> stand at the opening). Move if a better spot exists, then call step([0]) again to execute it.

messages.content: This confirms I'm well inside the bathroom - I can see the toilet, sink, shower area, and picture frame all around me. The bedroom with the bed is behind me. This is clearly the bathroom, matching the goal. I'll confirm the stop.

message.content: This confirms I'm well inside the bathroom - I can see the toilet, sink, shower area, and picture frame all around me. The bedroom with the bed is behind me. This is clearly the bathroom, matching the goal. I'll confirm the stop.
